newsletters.subject: How to build a company that withstands any era | Eric Ries, Lean Startup author
newsletters.author: Lenny Rachitsky
newsletters.relevance_reason: General business/startup methodology podcast with Eric Ries. Covers corporate governance, founder dynamics, and company building principles. Tangential to AI/LLM/Agent topics.

newsletters.subject: SpaceXAI's Spice Trade, Anthropic Targets the Trillion, and OpenAI's Stack Sweep
newsletters.author: Alex Banks
newsletters.relevance_reason: Covers major AI industry developments: Anthropic/SpaceX compute deal (300+ MW at Colossus data center, space computing interest), SpaceXAI merger (xAI dissolved into SpaceX), OpenAI IPO targeting $1T valuation, Anthropic at $380B valuation. Directly impacts AI ecosystem landscape.
suggested_wiki_pages: entities/spacex.md - update with SpaceXAI merger and compute deal
entities/anthropic.md - update with valuation and infrastructure expansion
entities/openai.md - update with IPO preparations and enterprise stack
concepts/ai-compute-infrastructure.md - new page on data center deals and space computing

newsletters.subject: Trustworthy AI Is Really About Delegated Agency
newsletters.relevance_reason: Discusses AI agent identity, delegated authority, and trust frameworks for autonomous agents. Core topics for the AI agents wiki - covers the POP Framework (Permissions, Obligations, Prohibitions) for AI governance, NIST-cataloged BX-AI OS architecture, and the critical distinction between data access and action authority for AI agents.
suggested_wiki_pages: concepts/ai-agent-identity.md - new page on agent identity and delegated authority
concepts/ai-trustworthiness.md - update with POP framework and governance layers
entities/allen-martinez.md - new entity for BX-AI OS creator (NIST-cataloged)
